assertions.text: The vehicle was stationary (not moving) immediately before the collision.
There was a single impact during the collision.
The vehicle was struck by a heavy impact (implying a forceful collision from another object) rather than striking something else.
The vehicle was stationary and then the impact occurred (stationary before impact).

verdicts.evidence: The GPS speed shows the vehicle was moving before the impact: [gps speed was 45.0 km/h at t=10.0 s](T3) and [47.0 km/h at t=11.0 s (impact window)][T5], while compute_stats reports a first_value of [0.0 km/h at t=0.0 s and last_value 2.0 km/h at t=16.0 s] (T2). The detect_events listing records a [speed_drop from 45.0 km/h to 22.0 km/h between t=10.0 and t=13.0 s](T3).

Detect_events found a single dominant impact spike on accel_x at [peak_t 11.01 s magnitude 5.86 g](T3) and one on accel_y at [peak_t 11.01 s magnitude 2.67 g](T3); these are concurrent and recorded within the same short interval t=[10.99,11.03] s giving a single collision event (T3, T2).

IMU shows large impact spikes on axes: [accel_x peak_signed_dev -5.862 g at t=11.01 s](T2) and [accel_y peak_signed_dev -2.665 g at t=11.01 s](T2), but device-to-vehicle axis alignment is undocumented so sensors alone cannot determine if the vehicle was struck or struck another object; per axis-caution this is unverifiable (T1, T2).

The accelerometer recorded a very large peak on axis x of [5.86 g at t=11.01 s](T3) (compute_stats peak_abs_dev [5.862 g at t=11.01 s](T2)), and a substantial integrated delta-V on that axis of [54.4 km/h](T2) indicating a heavy/severe impact (device-frame integral) (T2, T3).

GPS speed samples show the vehicle was moving into the interval before impact: [gps speed 42.0 km/h at t=9.0 s rising to 45.0 km/h at t=10.0 s and 47.0 km/h at t=11.0 s (impact window)](T4, T5) and detect_events recorded a [speed_drop from 45.0 km/h to 22.0 km/h between t=10.0 and t=13.0 s](T3), which contradicts the claim that the vehicle was stationary immediately before impact.

result.rationale: Telemetry shows a single, very large impact spike around [t=11.01 s with peak 5.86 g](T3)/(T2) and a concurrent GPS speed drop from [45.0 km/h to ~22.0 km/h starting at t=10.0 s](T3), indicating the vehicle was moving before impact; axis alignment prevents attributing struck/striking direction.

result.notes: 100 Hz IMU over a 16 s window. Units: accel g, gyro deg/s, speed km/h. Device-to-vehicle axis alignment is NOT documented; do not assume which axis is longitudinal without cross-checking against GPS speed.

accel_x.integrated_delta_v_note: integral of (a - baseline) over the range; device-frame axis, sign/orientation not vehicle-aligned

accel_y.integrated_delta_v_note: integral of (a - baseline) over the range; device-frame axis, sign/orientation not vehicle-aligned

accel_z.integrated_delta_v_note: integral of (a - baseline) over the range; device-frame axis, sign/orientation not vehicle-aligned

detections.note: direction ambiguous: device axis orientation undocumented

result.thresholds_doc: impact spike: |a-baseline|>2.5g (>=20ms, merged<0.25s); sustained: 0.5s-avg in 0.35-1.2g; rotation: >40.0deg/s for 0.3s; net rotation >60.0deg; speed drop: >=15.0km/h in <=3.0s; brake: 0->1 transitions; CISS impact: max |crash-pulse delta-V|

result.downsampling: per-bucket sample with the largest |deviation from channel median| is kept
per-bucket sample with the largest |deviation from channel median| is kept
